LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, OCT-14-2020 HEARING HELD RE: PLAINTIFF KIMBERLY STEUBEN'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION OF THE JUDGE PRO TEM IS ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO ITS RENEWAL AS TO DEFENDANT'S SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANT AND ITS COUNSEL IN THE AMOUNT OF $3,000 TO BE PAID WITHIN 15 DAYS AFTER NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THE COURT'S ORDER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-14-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. NICHOLAS F. SCARDIGLI, Esq. of Mayall Hurley (Tel: 209-477-3833) for Plaintiff Kimberly Steuben;
Atty. TIMOTHY B. NELSON, Esq. of Medina McKelvey LLP (Tel: 916-960-2211) for Defendant Storer Transportation Service.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF KIMBERLY STEUBEN's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Request For Sanctions.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories is denied without prejudice to its renewal as to Defendant's supplemental responses. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant and its counsel in the amount of $3,000 to be paid within 15 days after notice of entry of the court's order.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
